brief_document.title: Signal NIFs (receive_signal, send_signal)
brief_document.purpose: Implement the signal NIFs that workflow code calls to receive external signals and send signals to other workflows. Each NIF goes through the durability handoff: on replay, recorded signal events are returned without delivering or waiting for real signals; on live execution, the NIF delegates to AT's ConcreteSignalRouter (D4) and records durable events through the Recorder. Signals are the primary mechanism for external interaction with a running workflow (approvals, cancellations, data updates).
brief_document.task: Create nif_signal.rs with production NIF implementations for receive_signal/2 and send_signal/3. Each NIF constructs a NifContext (NB-001), builds the appropriate Command, calls resolve_or_execute_live. On replay, return the recorded result. On live, delegate to AT's ConcreteSignalRouter, record events through the Recorder (CO2), and return the result. Replace the stubs in engine_nifs.rs.

requirements.title: receive_signal/2 blocks until signal arrives and records SignalReceived
requirements.spec: WHEN receive_signal/2 is called on the live path, THE SYSTEM SHALL block the dirty NIF thread until a signal with the given name arrives (delivered by ConcreteSignalRouter) and record SignalReceived through the Recorder (CO2). WHEN called during replay, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the recorded signal payload without waiting for a real signal (C21).
acceptance: nif_signal.rs implements receive_signal_impl that takes a signal name and optional timeout, constructs a NifContext, resolves the receive-signal Command, and on ResumeLive delegates to AT's ConcreteSignalRouter signal-wait path.
On the live path, the NIF blocks (via Handle::block_on on a signal-wait future) until the signal arrives, then records SignalReceived with the signal name and payload.
On the replay path, the recorded SignalReceived payload is returned immediately.

A test pre-populates a history with SignalReceived, constructs a NifContext, and asserts the replay path returns the recorded payload; a live-path test asserts SignalReceived is recorded after delivery.

requirements.title: send_signal/3 delivers signal and records SignalSent
requirements.spec: WHEN send_signal/3 is called on the live path, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver the signal to the target workflow through AT's ConcreteSignalRouter and record SignalSent through the Recorder (CO2). WHEN called during replay, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the recorded result without delivering a real signal (C21).
acceptance: nif_signal.rs implements send_signal_impl that takes a target WorkflowId, signal name, and payload, constructs a NifContext, resolves the send-signal Command, and on ResumeLive delegates to AT's ConcreteSignalRouter signal-delivery path.
On the live path, SignalSent is recorded with the target workflow_id, signal name, and payload.
On the replay path, the recorded SignalSent result is returned without delivering.
A test asserts SignalSent is recorded on the live path; a replay test asserts the recorded result is returned without router interaction.

requirements.title: Signal NIFs delegate to ConcreteSignalRouter, not reimplemented routing
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL delegate all signal routing to AT's ConcreteSignalRouter (D4). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT reimplement signal delivery, buffering, or non-resident handoff in the NIF bridge.
acceptance: nif_signal.rs imports crate::signal::ConcreteSignalRouter (or its trait) and calls its delivery/wait methods.
No signal buffering, routing table, or delivery retry logic exists in nif_signal.rs.
The ConcreteSignalRouter handles both resident and non-resident workflow delivery; the NIF does not distinguish these cases.

requirements.title: engine_nifs.rs wiring for signal NIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL replace the receive_signal/2 and send_signal/3 stub entries in engine_nifs.rs with calls to nif_signal.rs implementations.
acceptance: The NIF functions registered in engine_nif_entries() for receive_signal/2 and send_signal/3 delegate to nif_signal implementations.
Both signal NIFs are registered as dirty (receive_signal blocks on signal arrival; send_signal blocks on delivery via block_on).
The stub {error, not_yet_implemented} responses are removed for these two NIFs.

boundaries: SHALL NOT reimplement signal routing, delivery, buffering, or non-resident handoff — those are AT's ConcreteSignalRouter (D4).
SHALL NOT bypass the Recorder to call EventStore::append directly (CO2).
SHALL NOT bypass the Resolver to skip replay checks (CO1).
SHALL NOT modify the ConcreteSignalRouter or SignalResumeHandoff types.
SHALL NOT import beamr outside the runtime module (CO8).
verification: Live path records SignalReceived and SignalSent events.
Replay path returns recorded results without signal interaction.

checklist.text: receive_signal/2 NIF blocks until a signal arrives and records SignalReceived.
send_signal/3 NIF delivers a signal to a target workflow and records SignalSent.
Signal NIFs on replay return recorded results without delivering or waiting for real signals.

stories.text: As a workflow author, I want to receive signals from external callers so that my workflow can react to events like approvals, cancellations, or data updates.

constraints.text: Every NIF goes through resolve_or_execute_live (or its equivalent replay check). No NIF may execute a side effect without first checking recorded history.
All event recording goes through the workflow's single-writer Recorder. No NIF calls EventStore::append directly.
Deterministic execution: workflow.now returns recorded timestamp, workflow.random is seeded from WorkflowId+RunId. No wall clock or entropy source in NIF-visible paths.
The aion crate remains transport-agnostic. Remote worker dispatch stays in aion-server's WorkerActivityDispatcher; the NIF bridge calls the ActivityDispatcher trait, not the concrete server type.
unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in any new code.
Only the runtime module imports beamr. NIF bridge code uses RuntimeHandle, not beamr directly.
Tests run against InMemoryStore. No test requires a database or live server.

resolved_context.intention: Connect the last mile: the BEAM process calls aion_flow_ffi NIFs, and those NIFs go through the durability handoff so every side effect is recorded, replayed on recovery, and deterministic. When this cluster lands, a workflow that starts, runs activities on remote workers, sleeps, receives signals, spawns children, and completes produces a full, replayable event history — the Temporal developer expectation, not just for basic cases, but for all of them.
